subject: general-knowledge
description: Regional Groups MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Which of the following countries joined the EU on 1 July 2013?
mcqs.explanation: Croatia joined the EU on 1 July 2013 (note: the source incorrectly states 2012 — actual date was 2013).

mcqs.q: The total number of EU member countries currently is:
mcqs.explanation: After the UK left the EU (Brexit, 31 Jan 2020), the EU has 27 member states.

mcqs.q: Which of the following was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2012?
mcqs.explanation: The European Union was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2012 for its contribution to peace and reconciliation in Europe.

mcqs.q: How many official languages are there in the European Union?
mcqs.explanation: The EU has 24 official languages (the source states 23 — the figure was updated when Croatian was added in 2013).

mcqs.q: How many official languages were there in the original 'Europe of Six' (1952-73)?
mcqs.explanation: The original Europe of Six (Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, Luxembourg, Netherlands) had 4 official languages.

mcqs.q: How many working languages are there in the EU?
mcqs.explanation: The EU has 3 working languages: English, French and German.

mcqs.q: The Translation Centre for the Bodies of the European Union, based in Luxembourg, provides translation services for:
mcqs.explanation: The Translation Centre in Luxembourg serves EU decentralised agencies (the source's official answer).

mcqs.q: When did French minister Aristide Briand suggest the idea of a European Federation within the League of Nations?
mcqs.explanation: Aristide Briand proposed a European Federation idea in 1929.

mcqs.q: In 1949 the Council of Europe was established by ten countries. Which of these was NOT a member?
mcqs.explanation: Germany was not among the 10 founding members of the Council of Europe in 1949 (it joined later in 1950).

mcqs.q: In 1950 the French Minister René Pleven developed the design for the EDC. What was his department?
mcqs.explanation: René Pleven was France's Minister of Defense; the EDC was the European Defence Community.

mcqs.q: The European Parliament is the co-legislator together with:
mcqs.explanation: The European Parliament co-legislates with the Council of the European Union under the ordinary legislative procedure.

mcqs.q: How many countries have so far had presidents of the European Commission?
mcqs.explanation: Per the source, 8 countries have had European Commission presidents.

mcqs.q: Who was the President of the Commission which resigned in 1999?
mcqs.explanation: Jacques Santer's Commission resigned in March 1999 amid a corruption scandal.

mcqs.q: What is the acronym for the Commission's European Anti-Fraud Office?
mcqs.explanation: OLAF — Office européen de Lutte Anti-Fraude (European Anti-Fraud Office).

mcqs.q: Who was the first president of the European Parliament?
mcqs.explanation: Robert Schuman, the French statesman, was the first president of the European Parliament (1958-1960).

mcqs.q: Where is the Secretariat-General of the European Parliament located?
mcqs.explanation: The Secretariat-General of the European Parliament is located in Luxembourg.

mcqs.q: Which country has the greatest number of seats in the European Parliament?
mcqs.explanation: Germany — the EU's most populous member — has the greatest number of seats in the European Parliament.

mcqs.q: The European Parliament appoints the:
mcqs.explanation: The European Parliament appoints the European Ombudsman.

mcqs.q: When did the first direct elections for the European Parliament take place?
mcqs.explanation: The first direct EP elections were held in June 1979 across 9 member states.

mcqs.q: Which region of the EU is the first to celebrate New Year?
mcqs.explanation: France's overseas territories (French Polynesia etc.) are among the first to enter the New Year.

mcqs.q: The world's oldest national anthem belongs to which EU member?
mcqs.explanation: The Wilhelmus — the Dutch national anthem — is the world's oldest national anthem (16th century).

mcqs.q: When did the Iberian Peninsula countries (Spain and Portugal) join the EU?
mcqs.explanation: Spain and Portugal joined the EU on 1 January 1986.

mcqs.q: French is an official language in Belgium, Luxembourg and which other EU country?
mcqs.explanation: French is an official language in France (along with Belgium and Luxembourg).

mcqs.q: Jean Monnet was one of the founding fathers of the European Community. Which member state was he from?
mcqs.explanation: Jean Monnet (1888-1979), considered a 'Father of Europe', was from France.

mcqs.q: Which country was the subject of the 1990 'enlargement' of the EU?
mcqs.explanation: German reunification (1990) effectively brought East Germany into the EU — the '1990 enlargement'.

mcqs.q: Which country was the only new member to join the EC in the 1981 enlargement?
mcqs.explanation: Greece was the only new member to join the EC in 1981.

mcqs.q: In terms of population and land area, which was the largest of the 2004 enlargement members?
mcqs.explanation: Poland was the largest of the 10 countries joining the EU in 2004 — both by area and population.

mcqs.q: Aside from Denmark, which EU member voted against adopting the Euro in a referendum?
mcqs.explanation: Sweden voted against adopting the Euro in a 2003 referendum.

mcqs.q: In 1973 the EC expanded to take in three new members: the UK, Denmark and:
mcqs.explanation: The 1973 EC enlargement brought in the UK, Denmark and Ireland.

mcqs.q: Where are the headquarters of the European Union?
mcqs.explanation: The EU has three institutional seats: Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg.

mcqs.q: Which 2004 EU enlargement member had a long-running border dispute involving a candidate country that did not join in 2004?
mcqs.explanation: Cyprus had (and has) the long-running division dispute with northern Cyprus/Turkey — and joined in 2004.

mcqs.q: In which country was the Treaty establishing the European Economic Community signed in 1957?
mcqs.explanation: The Treaty of Rome (1957) establishing the EEC was signed in Rome, Italy.

mcqs.q: Which 2004 enlargement country was once part of the Soviet Union?
mcqs.explanation: Lithuania (a Baltic state) was part of the Soviet Union before joining the EU in 2004.

mcqs.q: Which of these EU member states was a founder member of EFTA before joining the EC?
mcqs.explanation: Portugal was a founding member of EFTA (1960) before joining the EC in 1986.

mcqs.q: Which country has become the first-ever nation to leave the European Union?
mcqs.explanation: The United Kingdom left the EU on 31 January 2020 (Brexit) — the first-ever to leave.

mcqs.q: When was the European Monetary System (EMS) launched?
mcqs.explanation: The European Monetary System (EMS) was launched in March 1979.

mcqs.q: Who became the first president of the European Central Bank (ECB)?
mcqs.explanation: Wim Duisenberg of the Netherlands was the ECB's first president (1998-2003).

mcqs.q: When did the Euro banknotes and coins start to circulate?
mcqs.explanation: Euro banknotes and coins started circulating on 1 January 2002.

mcqs.q: Where is the European Central Bank located?
mcqs.explanation: The European Central Bank (ECB) is headquartered in Frankfurt, Germany.

mcqs.q: Which EU institution has seats in three cities — Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg?
mcqs.explanation: The European Parliament has seats in all three cities — Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg.

mcqs.q: Which country once 'froze' its application for EU membership?
mcqs.explanation: Malta froze its EU application in 1996 (after a change of government), reactivated 1998, joined 2004.

mcqs.q: What is the maximum number of MEPs in the European Parliament set by the Treaty of Nice?
mcqs.explanation: The Treaty of Nice (2001) capped the European Parliament at 732 MEPs.

mcqs.q: In which country is voting in European Parliament elections obligatory?
mcqs.explanation: Voting in European Parliament elections is obligatory in Belgium.

mcqs.q: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations), established on 8 August 1967, has its headquarters in:
mcqs.explanation: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 (cited as 9 Aug in some older sources) with HQ in Jakarta, Indonesia.

mcqs.q: Amnesty International was established in 1961 with its headquarters in London by a British lawyer named:
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International was founded by British lawyer Peter Benenson in 1961.

mcqs.q: In January 1992 ASEAN members agreed to establish a free trade area, cutting tariffs on non-agricultural goods over a period of:
mcqs.explanation: The ASEAN Free Trade Area (AFTA) was agreed in January 1992 with a 15-year tariff-reduction plan.

mcqs.q: Which international organization is effectively a cartel seeking to control prices and quantities of oil on the world market?
mcqs.explanation: OPEC (Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries) is the leading oil cartel.

mcqs.q: OPEC, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, was founded on:
mcqs.explanation: OPEC was founded on 14 September 1960 in Baghdad, Iraq.

mcqs.q: Where are the headquarters of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC)?
mcqs.explanation: OPEC is headquartered in Vienna, Austria (since 1965, after originally being in Geneva).

mcqs.q: What was the main objective behind the formation of OPEC?
options: To reduce production in order to increase prices
To control production and pricing of crude oil
To increase production in order to capture oil market
mcqs.explanation: OPEC's primary objective is to coordinate and unify petroleum policies — controlling production and pricing of crude oil.

mcqs.q: Which country suspended its OPEC membership in 2009?
mcqs.explanation: Indonesia suspended its OPEC membership in January 2009 (briefly reactivated in 2016, then re-suspended in November 2016).

mcqs.q: Which of the following statements regarding OPEC is NOT correct?
options: Its membership is open to every country
It was formed to control production and pricing of crude oil
mcqs.explanation: OPEC membership is restricted — only countries with substantial net oil exports can join. Saying it is 'open to every country' is incorrect.

mcqs.q: What is the total number of OPEC member countries (per source)?
mcqs.explanation: Per the original source, OPEC had 14 members (current membership has fluctuated; presently ~12 after Angola left in 2024).
